params.text:
Why a proper assessment matters

Because incontinence has different types and many possible causes, it should always be understood through a proper assessment rather than an assumption. Guessing the cause — or simply reaching for pads — can miss a treatable problem and leave a person struggling for longer than they need to.

  • Find the cause: assessment looks for why the incontinence is happening.
  • Rule out illness: it can uncover infection, constipation or other conditions.
  • Plan support: it leads to a personal plan, not a one-size-fits-all approach.

Led by the right professional

A continence assessment is carried out by a nurse or continence advisor. It considers the person's medical history, medicines, fluids, diet, mobility and daily routine, and often uses a record of how much someone drinks and passes over a few days. From this the professional can identify the type of incontinence, look for causes that can be treated, and agree a plan that may include exercises, changes to routine, treatment, or the right products for that person.

Your part is just as important. The information you notice and record — when leaks happen, how often, and anything that seems to trigger them — helps make the assessment accurate. Incontinence should never be treated as inevitable; with a proper assessment, many people can be cured or greatly improved.
